seoTitle: How to Integrate Shiprocket Checkout With Shopify
metaDescription: Speed up shipping at checkout by integrating Shiprocket with Shopify. Here's the setup process, from account creation to testing the connection.
intro: Offering real-time shipping rates and faster fulfillment at checkout can noticeably improve conversion rates, and Shiprocket is built to plug directly into Shopify for that purpose. This guide covers setting up a Shiprocket account, installing the app on Shopify, and configuring your shipping settings. It's aimed at store owners looking to speed up and automate their shipping workflow.

steps.heading: Set up your Shiprocket account
steps.body: Before connecting anything to Shopify, create and configure your Shiprocket account with the details it needs to manage shipping on your behalf.

steps.heading: Install the Shiprocket app on Shopify
steps.body: Add the Shiprocket app from the Shopify app listing so the two platforms can communicate.

steps.heading: Configure your shipping settings
steps.body: Set up shipping rules and preferences within the integration so checkout reflects accurate, real-time shipping rates and options.

steps.heading: Test the integration
steps.body: Run through a test order to confirm shipping rates and fulfillment data are flowing correctly between Shopify and Shiprocket before going live.

faq.question: What does Shiprocket add to a Shopify checkout?
faq.answer: It provides real-time shipping rates and faster shipping options at checkout, which can improve customer experience and conversion rates.

faq.question: Does the Shiprocket integration automate shipping notifications?
faq.answer: Yes, the compatibility between Shiprocket and Shopify enables automated shipping notifications and management, reducing manual work and errors.

faq.question: Is Shiprocket suitable for small Shopify stores?
faq.answer: Yes, the setup is described as beginner-friendly, making it accessible for both small boutiques and larger e-commerce operations.

faq.question: How do I know the integration is working correctly?
faq.answer: Testing the integration with a sample order before going live is the recommended way to confirm shipping rates and data are syncing properly.
